objective: Implement the second #298 child slice: make governed repository lifecycle and governance classifications machine-checkable in the registry schema and validator.
scope_boundary: Extend docs/governed_repos.json with explicit lifecycle_status, governance_status, and classification evidence for existing registry entries.
Extend docs/schemas/governed-repos.schema.json to require the classification contract.
Update governed repo loader and validator code to enforce classification fields without relying on prose.
Update org inventory drift logic so explicitly archived registry rows are no longer reported as unclassified drift.
Add focused validator tests for valid, missing, and malformed classifications.
Update registry contract documentation for the new classification fields.
Add issue-backed planning, exception, execution-scope, backlog, and progress artifacts for #310.
out_of_scope: Adding seek-and-ponder or EmailAssistant to the registry, which remains #311 and #312.
Reconciling README or STANDARDS generated/manual repository surfaces, which remains #313.
Claiming final org-wide governance e2e completion.
research_summary: Issue #298 established that every active org repository must be represented in governance and that omission cannot be used as an exemption mechanism. Issue #309 made live inventory drift visible. This slice adds the missing machine-readable classification layer so governed, limited, exempt, adoption-blocked, and archived states are explicit registry data with owner, rationale, review date, and issue evidence.

sprints.name: Classification Contract
sprints.goal: Represent lifecycle and governance state as required registry data rather than prose-only notes.
tasks: Add lifecycle_status enum values active and archived.
Add governance_status enum values governed, limited, exempt, and adoption_blocked.
Require classification.owner, classification.rationale, classification.review_date, and classification.issue_refs.
Populate the existing six registry entries without adding new repositories.
acceptance_criteria: All current registry entries include explicit lifecycle and governance classification fields.
ASC-Evaluator remains limited rather than silently absent or fully governed.
The registry schema rejects entries missing classification evidence.

sprints.name: Validator Enforcement
sprints.goal: Make local and CI validation fail closed when registry classifications are missing or malformed.
tasks: Update scripts/overlord/governed_repos.py to expose classification fields.
Update scripts/overlord/validate_governed_repos.py with enum, date, and issue-ref checks.
Update scripts/overlord/check_org_repo_inventory.py to consume archived lifecycle classification.
Add focused tests for missing classification, invalid status values, empty issue refs, bad review dates, non-boolean subsystems, unknown properties, and archived inventory classification.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: org inventory drift tests pass after fixture classification updates.
Final AC: negative-control fixture tests fail for missing or malformed classification data.

sprints.name: Final E2E Proof
sprints.goal: Prove #310 is compatible with #298's live-org governance loop before PR publication.
tasks: Run the governed repo validator against the real registry.
Run live org inventory drift in warn-only mode to prove this slice does not claim intake completion.
Run structured plan, execution-scope, backlog sync, Local CI Gate, py_compile, and diff hygiene checks.
Open a PR for #310 with validation evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: Local CI Gate passes for changed paths.
Final AC: live org inventory warn-only output still reports seek-and-ponder and EmailAssistant pending #311/#312.
Final AC: #310 remains open unless PR and e2e evidence are linked.
Final AC: parent #298 remains open until final #314 e2e closeout.

specialist_reviews.focus: Registry schema semantics, validator fail-closed behavior, test coverage, and #298/#309/#311/#312 boundary alignment.
specialist_reviews.summary: Initial review found blockers in #309 fixture compatibility, schema/validator equivalence, raw boolean validation, archived-repo detector behavior, and registry contract documentation. The implementation was revised to address them before PR publication.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude working-tree re-review accepted the schema/validator/inventory/doc scope with follow-up notes. The loader type-safety note was fixed immediately; the schema additionalProperties note was verified against the current schema and is already satisfied.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #310 may add registry classification fields for existing entries, schema and validator enforcement, focused tests, planning/status artifacts, same-family exception, and implementation execution scope. It may not add missing org repos or edit downstream repositories.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement classification schema enforcement, run final e2e commands, complete alternate-family review, then open PR for #310.

material_deviation_rules: If seek-and-ponder or EmailAssistant registry intake is needed, stop and move that work to #311 or #312.
If README or STANDARDS governed repo surfaces need reconciliation, stop and move that work to #313.
If alternate-family review rejects the classification contract, revise before PR publication.
If live inventory drift unexpectedly clears, verify #311/#312 landed on main before updating #310 evidence.
If final e2e commands are skipped, do not publish.
approved_by: operator instruction: follow repo rules, no HITL, e2e testing as final AC
